title: Elementor Website Builder (Full-Time)
description:
We run a web design agency in the US that builds websites for home service contractors. Since 2014, we have worked with a team in the Philippines, and value the culture very much. 



We currently need a full-time Elementor builder to produce client websites from designs that are already approved.



This is not a design role. You will not be making creative decisions or choosing layouts. You will receive a fully approved website design as a live HTML preview you can view in your browser, along with all the written content, images, brand colors, and fonts. Your job is to take that approved design and rebuild it in WordPress using Elementor Pro. Accurately, efficiently, and with clean structure.



The design is done. You are the person who brings it to life in Elementor.





What you will do every day:



- Open an approved website preview side by side with Elementor and build the homepage to match it as closely as possible

- Copy and paste pre-written content into each section (all copy is provided to you)

- Upload and place images into the correct sections (all images are provided and sized)

- Set up the color palette, fonts, and spacing to match the approved design

- Build inner pages (About, Services, Contact, Service Area) following the same design style

- Make sure every page is fully mobile responsive

- Use proper heading structure: one H1 per page, H2s for sections, H3s for subsections

- Test the site on desktop, tablet, and mobile before marking it complete

- Make revisions when our team or the client requests changes





What we give you for every project:



- A live preview URL showing the exact approved design

- All page content (headlines, body text, CTAs) written and ready to paste in

- All images sized and ready to upload

- Brand colors as hex codes and font names

- A structured handoff document listing everything you need

- Pre-built Elementor template sections you can import and customize





You need to have:



- At least 2 years of hands-on experience building websites in WordPress with Elementor Pro

- Strong attention to detail. You can look at a design and match it closely in Elementor without needing someone to hold your hand through it.

- Solid understanding of responsive design. Sites need to look great on all screen sizes.

- Understanding of basic SEO structure. Proper heading hierarchy (H1, H2, H3), alt text on images, clean page structure.

- Reliable internet and a computer that can run WordPress smoothly

- Ability to work during or overlap with US Central Time hours

- Good English communication. You will get instructions and give updates through chat.





Bonus (not required):



- Experience with Elementor template kits or reusable section templates

- Experience building sites for home service companies like contractors, plumbers, or roofers

- Knowledge of page speed optimization (image compression, keeping plugins minimal)

- Familiarity with WordPress plugins like Yoast SEO, WPForms, Smush, or ShortPixel





What this role is not:



- Not a graphic design job. You will not be designing layouts from scratch.

- Not a content writing job. All copy is written before it reaches you.

- Not a development job. No custom PHP, no theme development, no coding.

- Not a one-time project. This is ongoing full-time work with a steady flow of sites to build.





How our process works:



1. Our design tool generates a website preview and the client approves it

2. Our content team writes all the page copy

3. We put together a handoff package with the preview, content, images, and brand details

4. You build it in Elementor (target is 2 to 4 hours per homepage, 1 to 2 hours per inner page)

5. Our team reviews your build against the approved design

6. You make any adjustments needed

7. Site goes live



We expect to start with 2 to 3 websites per week and grow from there. This is a long-term position for someone who wants stable, consistent work with a growing agency.



Pay: $800 to $1,200 USD per month depending on experience and speed. Paid weekly through Wise or PayPal.



Schedule: Full-time, 40 hours per week. Hours are flexible but you need overlap with US Central Time for communication.





How to apply:



Do not send a generic application. Include the following:



1. A link to 2 or 3 websites you personally built in Elementor.

2. How many years you have been using Elementor specifically.

3. On average, how long does it take you to build a homepage in Elementor when you already have the design and content provided?

4. What is your favorite Elementor feature and why?

5. Write the word "builder" at the top of your application so I know you read this entire post.



We read every application that follows these instructions. Looking forward to hearing from you.

title: Director of Digital Operations & AI Product Launch (Integrator)
description:
I’m building and scaling a multi-brand ecosystem across publishing, AI products, wellness, and curriculum. The ideas and assets exist. The problem is execution at scale.

I’m looking for a senior-level operator who can own the execution of multiple tech-heavy, revenue-focused projects—from AI chatbots and digital funnels to telehealth and curriculum platforms.

If you’ve run complex digital launches, managed cross-functional teams, and turned chaos into shipped products and recurring revenue, keep reading.

You are not a VA, “online assistant,” or someone who only takes orders.

You are the person who can take goals ? design a plan ? drive people and tech until it’s live and producing results.

________________________________________

Core Projects You’ll Help Execute (First 6–12 Months)

You won’t do every task yourself—but you’ll be responsible for making sure these move from “in progress” to “live and producing”:

• Thug Series GPT – AI assistant + funnel for a bestselling Street Lit universe.

• SmartClient Bots – AI/automation builds for clients using tools like GoHighLevel and GPT.

• MyCondolences – Tech-enabled condolence/memorial service (potential pet pivot).

• Walk In Wellness Virtual Clinic / TPA – Telehealth front-end, intake, and provider onboarding systems.

• Transform Curriculum – Digital/self-paced curriculum portal for schools and programs.

• NWNOI Funnel Launch – Donations + book sales + simple nurturing flows.

• Publishing CEO / New Book Releases – Launch systems for new titles.

• W.C. Global Strategy Offers – Strategy/consulting offers with clear funnels and onboarding.

________________________________________

Key Outcomes (First 90 Days)

In your first 90 days, success looks like:

• Clarity & Plan

o Each of the priority projects above has:

? A simple, written roadmap

? Defined milestones

? Owners for each task (you, me, VAs, vendors)

• Shipped Assets

o At least 2–3 offers/funnels are live and capable of collecting leads and payments (e.g., W.C. Global Strategy offer, SmartClient Bots, Thug Series GPT beta).

• Operational Rhythm

o A weekly reporting system:

? What was done

? What’s blocked

? What’s next

o Clear expectations for VAs and contractors.

I’m not interested in “activity.” I’m interested in shipped, working systems.

________________________________________

Responsibilities

• Project & Operations Leadership

o Turn high-level goals into clear project plans, timelines, and weekly priorities.

o Coordinate across me, designers, VAs, devs, and specialists.

o Keep multiple projects moving without dropping details.

• Digital & AI Product Execution

o Oversee the setup of AI tools (GPT-based products, bots, automation flows).

o Work with technical implementers to configure and test:

? Chatbots / GPT flows

? GoHighLevel automations

? Landing pages, payment links, and onboarding sequences.

• Launch & Funnel Management

o Plan and oversee launches for:

? Consulting offers (W.C. Global Strategy)

? Book and IP-based products

? AI-based services (SmartClient Bots, Thug Series GPT)

o Coordinate
 campaigns, simple ads, and basic analytics.

• Systems & Documentation

o Create and refine SOPs so projects can be handed off and scaled.

o Clean up and simplify our existing project trackers so everyone knows what matters now.

• Reporting & Accountability

o Run a weekly check-in:

? Status by project

? Bottlenecks and decisions needed

? Risks and tradeoffs

o Hold tea
mbers (and me) accountable to agreed priorities.

________________________________________

Must-Haves

• Proven experience running multi-project digital operations:

o Agency, online education, SaaS, or info-product background is ideal.

• You’ve launched offers/funnels before:

o You understand the basic pieces: offer ? landing page ? checkout ? delivery ? follow-up.

• Strong familiarity with AI + automation tools, such as:

o ChatGPT/GPT-based products

o GoHighLevel (or similar CRMs/automation tools)

o Basic API/automation logic (you don’t need to write code, but you need to think like a builder).

• You can plan AND execute:

o You’re comfortable writing a roadmap and then getting into the tools or directing others to finish.

• You are direct, organized, and comfortable in a fast-moving environment.

o This is not a corporate, slow-moving culture.

________________________________________

Nice-to-Haves

• Experience in any of these domains:

o Publishing / authors / book launches

o Mental health / telehealth / wellness services

o Curriculum / EdTech

• Experience managing:

o Remote teams

o VAs and specialized freelancers

o Light ad spend (Meta, Amazon Ads, Google)

________________________________________

Working Style & Expectations

• Remote, flexible, but with agreed core hours for communication.

• You’re comfortable with voice/video check-ins and written updates.

• You bring solutions, not just problems:

o “Here’s the issue, here are 2–3 options, here’s my recommendation.”

________________________________________

How to Apply



Step 1 – Loom Video (Required)

Send a 3–5 minute Loom video where I can see you and hear you clearly in English. In the video, cover:



Who you are & your background



Brief intro (who you are, what you do, what types of clients/businesses you’ve worked with).



The most complex digital/AI product or funnel you’ve helped ship



What the product/funnel was.



What tools were involved (GPT/AI, GoHighLevel or similar, automations, platforms).



Your exact role and what you personally owned and drove to completion.



Any numbers or concrete outcomes (launch size, revenue, leads, show-up rate, etc., if you can share).



Why you’re a fit for this kind of role



How you handle multiple projects at once.



How you keep yourself and a team organized and accountable.



Your availability (part
-time) and ideal monthly retainer range.



Please share the Loom link in your
 (no attachments).



Step 2 –
 with Supporting Details



Along with your Loom link,
 me with:



A link to your LinkedIn profile.



2–3 examples (or short descriptions) of relevant projects you’ve worked on:



Launches



Automations



AI tools / GPT products



Funnels / offers you helped ship



Your typical monthly retainer range for this level of responsibility (part-time or full-time) and your earliest start date.

title: Paid Ads Operator/ Media Buyer — Meta Ads + Google Ads (E-Commerce / DTC)
description:
We run multiple direct-to-consumer e-commerce brands on Shopify, scaling through paid Meta and Google advertising. We need a detail-oriented ads operator who can execute inside ad accounts with speed, accuracy, and an understanding of WHY things are structured the way they are.

This is an execution role, not a strategy role. You'll receive creative direction, campaign structures, and scaling decisions from the founder and creative team. Your job is to execute flawlessly inside Meta Ads Manager and Google Ads, upload creatives correctly, monitor performance daily, and flag issues fast.

What You'll Do:

— Upload static and video ad creatives into Meta Ads Manager following exact naming conventions and campaign structures provided

— Set up Dynamic Creative Tests (DCTs) and CBO campaigns with concept-based ad sets as directed

— Schedule ad launches correctly (midnight in ad account timezone)

— Duplicate winning ads and move them to scaling campaigns (Champions ad set structure)

— Set up and manage Google Ads campaigns (Performance Max, Search, Shopping)

— Monitor daily performance: ROAS, CPA, CTR, CPM, hook rate, hold rate, frequency, spend velocity

— Flag creative fatigue early (rising frequency, declining CTR, dropping ROAS)

— Provide concise daily or weekly performance summaries with clear call-outs on winners, losers, and what needs attention

— Track learning phase status and flag when too many ads are stuck in learning

— Maintain strict naming conventions and keep the ad account clean and organized

— Ensure creative diversity across the account (different formats, angles, avatars — not 15 variations of the same image)

Requirements (Non-Negotiable):

— 1–2+ years hands-on experience running Meta Ads for e-commerce or DTC brands (not lead gen, not local business, not boosted posts)

— Working experience with Google Ads (Performance Max and Shopping at minimum)

— Understands CBO vs ABO and when each is used

— Knows how to set up and read DCT breakdown data to identify winning creative variables

— Understands creative diversity and why similar-looking ads get penalized by Meta's algorithm

— Comfortable reading ad metrics: ROAS, CPA, CTR, CPM, hook rate, hold rate, frequency

— Familiar with broad targeting as a primary prospecting approach (no interest stacking)

— Fluent English (written communication must be clear)

— Reliable internet and available during some overlap with US Eastern timezone

— Works independently once given clear direction

Nice to Have:

— Experience with 
 or bulk ad upload tools

— Familiarity with Foreplay.co

— Understanding of ad-to-landing-page message match

— Experience managing multiple brands simultaneously

Compensation:

— $
/month base depending on experience and hours

— Performance bonuses tied to account KPIs

— Clear path to increased comp as brands scale

To Apply — You Must Include All of the Following:



A 2–3 sentence intro about your e-commerce ad experience

A screenshot or Loom video walkthrough of an ad account you've managed (blur sensitive data — we want to see structure, naming conventions, and organization)

Your timezone and daily availability

Your expected monthly rate

Answer this question: "An ad set in your testing campaign has been live for 3 days. One ad has taken 85% of the spend but ROAS is below target. The other two ads have barely spent. What do you do and why?"

title: Amazon Account Manager with PPC experience
description:
We are a UK-based e-commerce business managing multiple private-label brands on Amazon. Our growth is driven by sharp account management and advertising built on data - and we are now looking for an Amazon Account Manager and PPC Specialist to own both sides of that equation.

This is a full-time, long-term remote role. You will not just run ads. You will own the health and growth of one dedicated Amazon account - from listing quality and catalog hygiene to PPC strategy, keyword research, and weekly performance reporting with real actions attached.



What you will be doing

- Managing Amazon Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, and Sponsored Display campaigns end-to-end

- Owning full Amazon account health: listings, catalog integrity, Buy Box monitoring, reviews, and inventory flags

- Conducting keyword research, bid management, and search term harvesting on a continuous basis

- Monitoring and reporting on TACoS, ACoS, CTR, CVR, and Profit after PPC weekly - with clear actions, not just numbers

- Tracking BSR, Buy Box health, and inventory levels and connecting them directly to ad performance decisions

- Running bulk operations to manage campaigns at scale without dropping the ball on detail

- Supporting new product launches - from listing setup through to initial ranking strategy

- Identifying wasted spend, cannibalisation, budget caps, and stockout risks before they become problems

If you are serious about this role, start your cover letter with the words - data wins, not guesses.





Requirements

- Full-time availability, 40 hours per week, remote

- Minimum 2 years of hands-on Amazon account management experience - you must understand the full account, not just campaigns

- Strong, proven Amazon PPC skills - this is non-negotiable

- Solid grasp of key metrics: TACoS, ACoS, Profit after PPC, CVR, CTR, BSR

- Must-have tools - clearly state in your reply which of these you have used: Helium 10, Data Dive, Linnworks, Notion, Asana, Slack - and describe your experience with AI tools

- Proactive mindset - we want someone who flags problems and brings solutions, not someone who waits for an Asana task to appear in their queue

- Proof of results required - show us the accounts or campaigns you managed and what the numbers looked like before and after your work

title: Meta Ads Specialist
description:

At Simplified Impact, we help businesses grow through data-driven digital marketing strategies, with a strong focus on performance advertising. We manage and scale Meta 
 & Instagram) ad campaigns for clients, focusing on lead generation and revenue growth



We are looking for a Meta Ads Specialist who can take ownership of day-to-day campaign performance (from launch and optimization to testing and scaling) while working closely with our Paid Ads Lead on overall strategy..

This role is ideal for someone who already has hands-on experience managing Meta Ads, understands how to optimize for real business results (not just clicks), and enjoys working in a performance-driven environment.

Meta Ads Campaign Management & Optimization:

Work alongside the Paid Media Specialist to launch, manage, and optimize Meta 
 & Instagram) ad campaigns focused on lead generation and conversions. Analyze performance data and adjust budgets, audiences, and creatives to reach client goals.





Reporting & Performance Monitoring:

Monitor KPIs such as CPA/CPL, CTR, frequency, and conversion rates. Share performance insights and flag issues or opportunities for scaling.





Creative Testing & Ad Improvements:

Implement creative and audience testing. Suggest new angles and improve ad creatives using Canva when needed, following brand guidelines.





Ad Account Setup & Tracking Support:

Support ad account setup, including pixel installation, event tracking, and domain verification. Coordinate with developers or tea
mbers to ensure accurate tracking and data flow.





Lead Funnel &
 Support (As Needed):

Marketing Platform Support:

Work within platforms such as HubSpot, Klaviyo or Go High Level to support lead management and automation related to paid ads.





Required Skills and Experience:

2+ years hands-on experience managing Meta Ads with lead generation or conversion goals





Experience analyzing Ads Manager data and optimizing based on performance metrics





Basic design skills for creating and adjusting ad creatives using tools such as Canva (Photoshop experience is a plus, not required).





Experience or exposure to
 marketing tools such as HubSpot, Klaviyo, or Go High Level, particularly for follow-up campaigns and lead nurturing.





Understanding of lead generation fundamentals, including form-based leads, conversion tracking, and basic funnel concepts.





Strong attention to detail and ability to follow briefs and processes provided by the Paid Ad Specialist.





Ability to work collaboratively within a fast-paced team and support marketing initiatives targeting Western markets.





Desired Attributes:

Organized, reliable, and able to work with minimal supervision





Results-driven and comfortable working in a performance-focused environment





Good communication skills and ability to collaborate with a remote team





Interested in long-term growth in performance marketing





How to Apply:

If you’re interested in supporting high-performing Meta Ads campaigns and growing your skills in paid advertising, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your resume and any relevant examples of work (ad creatives, reports, or campaigns—if available)

title: Media Buyer
description:
Stop Running Ads. Start Engineering Profitable Customer Acquisition.



Media Buyer - Meta & Performance Marketing



Most media buyers optimize CPMs. You'll build scalable acquisition machines that print money while you sleep.



About Rescale Media



We're not just another e-commerce company. We're a brand incubator on a mission to build enduring, value-driven brands that bring meaningful products into families' lives.



We've scaled from just drop shipping to a 30+ person team doing $1M+/month. Our goal? Multi 9-figures. And we're just getting started.



???? Learn more at 
dia



Our Core Values



???? GENIUSES & OWNERS



We partner with builders who think like founders. Not employees waiting to be told what to do—owners who see a problem and fix it.



? ITERATIONS > HOURS



We don't measure success by time at your desk. We measure it by how fast you can test, learn, and improve. Ship it. Learn. Repeat.



???? TRUTH OVER EGO



We prioritize honesty and clarity, even when it's uncomfortable. No politics. No BS. Just data and results.



The Role



We spend serious money on Meta ads—and we need someone who treats every dollar like it's their own.



You won't just 'manage' campaigns. You'll partner with our Creative Strategists to test winning angles, analyze performance data to find insights others miss, and scale what works while killing what doesn't.



Our brands have product-market fit. Your job is to pour fuel on the fire—profitably.



What You'll Do



Manage and scale Meta ad campaigns across multiple brands

Analyze performance data daily and make rapid optimization decisions

Collaborate with Creative Strategy on ad concepts, hooks, and angles

Build and test audience strategies—LALs, interests, broad, and everything in between

Maintain ROAS targets while scaling spend

Create clear performance reports and insights for leadership

Stay ahead of platform changes and test new features



What We're Looking For



2+ years of hands-on Meta Ads experience

Proven track record of managing $500K+/month in ad spend profitably

Deep understanding of Meta's algorithm, attribution, and optimization

Strong analytical skills—you live in the data

Experience with creative testing frameworks (you know a hook from a headline)

E-commerce experience preferred (DTC brands a plus)



Bonus Points



Experience with $100K+/month spend

Background in creative strategy or copywriting

You've scaled campaigns from test budgets to 6-figure monthly spend

Experience with other platforms (TikTok, Google, Taboola) is nice but not required



This Role Is NOT For...



We're being direct because we respect your time:



Media buyers who just 'set and forget'

Those who blame the algorithm when things don't work

Anyone who can't explain WHY a campaign is working (or not)

People who aren't comfortable with high stakes and tight targets



Compensation & Benefits



???? Monthly Salary: $1,500/month



Plus:



Monthly performance bonuses tied to company Operating Profit (5-25% of salary)

Quarterly profit pool distribution (2-7.5% of company profits shared among team)

Remote-first environment (European timezone preferred)

Work with a team of A-players who actually give a damn

Direct founder access—no layers of bureaucracy

Real ownership of your domain—we don't micromanage



Our Hiring Process



We don't waste time—yours or ours. Here's how it works:



Step 1: Application (You're here!)

Step 2: Initial Interview with Founder

Step 3: Internal Assessment by Rescale

Step 4: WhatsApp update for next steps

Step 5: Signing of Independent Contractor and NDA

Step 6: Onboarding and Training



Total timeline: 1 week from application to offer.



Ready to Apply?



[  
  ]



Complete the application form above. Be thorough—this is your first impression.



P.S.



Our best media buyer once found a winning audience that nobody else was targeting. It scaled to 6-figures in spend. If you have stories like that, we want to hear them.



P.P.S.



If you're reading this far, you're already more thorough than 90% of applicants. That's exactly who we want.

title: Part-Time VA — Digital Product Business (Supply Chain / Ops Brand)
description:
We are looking for someone to start within the next 7 days. Priority given to applicants who complete the application requirements in full.



JOB TITLE: Part-Time VA — Digital Product Business (Supply Chain / Ops Brand)



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are a digital product and advisory brand in the consumer goods / supply chain space. We sell downloadable guides and offer consulting services to founders and brand operators.



We are looking for a part-time virtual assistant to manage the operational side of the business — content scheduling,
 platform monitoring, basic ad reporting, and a weekly summary report. This role is fully remote. You will work independently from a clear checklist. Approximately 5-8 hours per week.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

?  Schedule and publish social media content (LinkedIn + TikTok) using Buffer — 3 posts per week per platform. Scripts provided.

?  Monitor ConvertKit
 platform weekly — flag any deliverability issues, unusual unsubscribe spikes, or automation errors.

?  Monitor Gumroad weekly — compile sales data (products sold, revenue, refund requests).

?  Monitor Meta ad campaigns — compile spend, impressions, clicks, and CPL in a weekly report template provided.

?  Tag ConvertKit subscribers based on reply segments from
 4 (instructions provided).

?  Flag 'engaged-replied' and 'enterprise' subscribers in the weekly summary for the owner's personal follow-up.

?  Compile and deliver the Sunday Summary report each Sunday by 8:00 AM Eastern using the template provided.

?  Handle basic customer support (download issues, refund requests) using provided scripts. Escalate anything outside the script.



WHAT YOU WON'T DO

?  Write original content (scripts are provided — you schedule, not create)

?  Make any business decisions (a decision tree is provided — anything not covered gets escalated)

?  Handle media inquiries, partnership requests, or press (always forward to owner)

?  Respond to subscriber replies personally (flag them — owner responds)



TOOLS USED

?  Buffer (social scheduling) — training provided

?  ConvertKit 
 platform) — training provided

?  Gumroad (product delivery + sales) — training provided

?  Meta Ads Manager (read-only access for reporting) — training provided

?  Notion (operations hub + task tracking) — training provided

?  Google Sheets (Sunday Summary report) — must be comfortable with basic Sheets



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

?  2+ years of VA or online business operations experience

?  Comfortable with digital marketing tools 
 platforms, social scheduling, ad dashboards)

?  Strong written English — the Sunday Summary must be clear and professional

?  Reliable, proactive, and detail-oriented — you notice anomalies before they become problems

?  Available to deliver the Sunday Summary each Sunday by 8:00 AM Eastern



COMPENSATION

$8-10/hr depending on experience and platform. Approximately 5-8 hours per week. Paid weekly via Upwork or PayPal. 30-day trial period with review.



TO APPLY

Please include: (1) Your relevant experience with digital product businesses or online marketing tools. (2) Your availability and time zone. (3) One example of a weekly summary or report you have prepared for a previous client.



Applications without all three items will not be considered.

title: Lead Full Stack Developer (Audit & Delivery Oversight)
description:

- Provide accurate time estimates for new features and projects

Required Skills & Experience:

- Strong experience in ASP.NET / C# (Core / MVC)

- Experience working with REST APIs / third-party integrations (payments, shipping, etc.)

Essential Experience:

Desirable

What We’re Looking For

As a Copywriter, you will play a pivotal role in crafting compelling content and messaging across multiple platforms to engage our customers and drive sales. This position requires fluency in both US English and Filipino, along with the ability to write effective translations and captivating ad copy. If you have a passion for storytelling and a knack for persuasive communication, we want to hear from you!



Responsibilities:

 newsletters, and social media posts in both US English and Filipino.

Opportunity to lead copywriting projects and mentor junior tea
mbers.

title: Senior SOX Auditor (Business Process)
description:
Position Summary



The Sr. SOX Auditor (Business Process) will execute Sarbanes-Oxley internal audits, assessing internal controls for compliance and effectiveness. The role requires collaboration with U.S. based internal audit team, process owners, and management to strengthen internal controls and support enterprise risk management.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:



Lead and perform walkthroughs and detailed testing of internal controls over financial reporting (ICFR) under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX 404).

Evaluate the design and effectiveness of key controls, including business process controls and entity-level controls.

Identify control deficiencies, develop practical remediation plans with process owners, and track resolution.

Coordinate and maintain strong relationships with external auditors to align SOX testing plans and share testing results.

Prepare and review workpapers and testing documentation in accordance with PCAOB and IIA standards.

Assist in the annual risk assessment and scoping process for SOX compliance.

Provide guidance to business units on internal control best practices and control enhancements.

Support or lead other internal audit projects or special reviews, including operational, compliance, and investigative audits, as assigned.

Assist in the implementation and continuous improvement of governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) tools.

Participate in process improvement and automation initiatives related to SOX compliance.



QUALIFICATIONS:



Education and Certifications:



Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field.

CPA, CIA, or CISA certification preferred or actively pursuing.



Experience:



5–10 years of experience in internal audit, public accounting (Big 4 preferred), or a combination, with a strong focus on SOX 404 compliance.

Experience working in a publicly traded company environment is highly desirable.



Technical Skills:



Strong understanding of COSO Internal Control – Integrated Framework (2013).

Knowledge of PCAOB standards, SEC requirements, and SOX 404 compliance.

Proficient in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, and audit management tools.

Proficient with business process and internal controls over financial reporting.



Soft Skills:



Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills.

Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.

High level of integrity and professional skepticism.

Experience supporting audit teams based in the U.S.

title: Paralegal/Attorney – Criminal Defense
description:
Position Overview:

We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Paralegal or Attorney to support our criminal defense practice. This role requires in-depth knowledge of criminal law, including motion preparation, legal research, and document management. The ideal candidate will be responsible for preparing motions, drafting legal documents, and ensuring compliance with legal procedures. They will work closely with attorneys to ensure the defense strategy is executed effectively, and help prepare cases for court. This is a remote position requiring exceptional written English, organizational skills, and a strong grasp of U.S. criminal law procedures fundamentals.



Key Responsibilities:



• Draft, review, and be prepared to file various legal documents including motions, briefs, and memoranda.

• Prepare appendices for motions, including organizing exhibits, declarations, and supporting documentation.

• Prepare the table of contents for legal filings, ensuring accuracy and compliance with court rules.

• Conduct in-depth legal research on complex criminal law issues and generate a research summary for the attorney.

• Analyze discovery materials to identify critical issues and inconsistencies.

• Prepare memoranda and case summaries to aid in litigation strategy.

• Assist in the preparation of motions for hearings, trials, and appeals, including motions to suppress evidence, dismiss charges, or modify bail.

• Manage case files, ensuring all relevant documents and evidence are properly documented and stored.

• Prepare discovery responses, subpoenas, and other legal documents as directed.

• Coordinate with clients, witnesses, and experts to gather information and evidence for cases.

• Maintain communication with the court, opposing counsel, and clients regarding motions, hearings, and court dates.

• Provide support during trial preparation, including organizing exhibits, trial briefs, and witness lists.

• Ensure all legal documents comply with applicable state and federal rules and deadlines.



Qualifications:



• Must be a Graduate of a law school (JD).

• Proven experience in criminal defense law, specifically in motion practice.

• Strong knowledge of court rules, procedures, and legal writing standards.

• Experience preparing appendices, tables of contents, and organizing large case files.

• Excellent research, analytical, and writing skills.

• Must have strong knowledge of legal citation

• Ability to manage multiple cases and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

• Strong communication skills and attention to detail.

• Experience with legal research tools (e.g.,Lexis/Nexis and Westlaw) and document management software, such as Clio CRM software.



Preferred:



• Prior experience in criminal defense law or litigation.

• Knowledge of specific motions in criminal defense, including motions to suppress, dismiss, and motions for bond 

        reductions.

• Familiarity with Nevada local court rules and procedures in criminal defense.



Application Process:



Interested candidates should submit the following:

• An updated resume.

• A cover letter detailing your experience with U.S. criminal law and Clio software.

• Two writing samples (legal motions or briefs preferred) with how long it required the applicant to finalize the presented 

        document (in hours) from the time the assignment was given to the document presented.

Role Overview:

SEO:

description:

100% Remote | Part-Time | USD $6/hour + Performance Incentives 



ABOUT THIS ROLE

CinRetta Digital Marketing Services is looking for a detail-oriented and self-motivated Lead Generation & Skip Tracing Specialist to support one of our US-based real estate clients.

If you enjoy deep research, have a strong eye for data accuracy, and want to build your career in the short-term rental (STR) and real estate industry, this opportunity is for you.

This is a part-time, fully remote role paid in USD with performance incentives for strong output.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

- Lead Generation & List Building

- Build clean, structured lead lists that include owner names, phone numbers,
 addresses, and property addresses

Skip Tracing & Data Research

WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR

Required:

Nice to Have:

title: AI-Powered Video Editor
description:
About us

We are a UK-based e-commerce business selling home improvement and building materials products On eCommerce marketplaces. Our products solve real problems for homeowners -and we need someone who can turn that into compelling video content that educates, converts, and scales.

This is a remote, output-based role. We are not counting your hours. We are counting your videos.





What you will be doing

-Researching existing content and competitor videos for each product, then using AI tools to build original, on-brand videos around our products

-Creating educational, benefit-driven content -how to install, why it works, what makes it better, and how a homeowner can do it themselves

-Adding English also Polish  language voiceover to all videos -clear, natural, and suited to the platform

-Delivering a consistent weekly volume of finished videos ready to post -the more output, the better

-Iterating based on feedback until the format and style is locked, then scaling that formula

If you are serious about this role, start your application with the words -content that converts.





Requirements

-Proven experience editing video for social media or e-commerce -portfolio required, show us real work

-Strong working knowledge of AI video tools -clearly state in your reply which tools you use: CapCut, Runway, HeyGen, ElevenLabs, Pictory, or others

-Ability to produce clean English voiceover -either your own voice or AI-generated, but it must sound natural and professional

-Self-driven and output-focused -this role is measured by videos delivered per week, not hours logged

-Ability to work independently from a brief, research the product, and produce without hand-holding

title: Video Editor – Experience Working With Major Brands (3+ Years)
description:
EDITOR — VIDEO AD CREATIVES

Niche: Beauty / Skincare (Korean-inspired Anti-Aging)

We're looking for a Performance Editor to cut high-converting video ad creatives for Meta 
 & Instagram), primarily from UGC footage, hooks, and raw content.

Our brand is in the beauty and skincare space and we are only hiring editors with 2+ years of experience in performance video editing, direct response ads, or social media content editing.

This is NOT a "make things look cinematic" job. This is a marketing-first editing role. Every cut, every caption, every transition exists to stop the scroll and drive the click.

You will receive from our Creative Strategist: briefs, hooks, raw footage, UGC clips, voiceovers, and reference ads. Your job is to turn these into multiple creative variations built for testing and scaling.

WHAT YOU WILL DO

You will edit video ad creatives for Meta feeds, stories, and reels. You will cut hook variations from the same raw footage. You will add captions, motion text, and on-screen callouts that drive clarity and attention. You will produce multiple variations from a single brief. You will maintain a fast revision turnaround and keep files organized. You will work closely with the Creative Strategist — briefs come from them, performance feedback loops back to them.

HOW WE EVALUATE YOUR WORK

We look at whether you can make the first 2-3 seconds impossible to scroll past. We look at whether you can turn one piece of footage into multiple distinct variations. We look at whether you prioritize message clarity over visual complexity. We look at whether you can follow a brief precisely and iterate fast. We look at whether you produce volume without sacrificing quality.

EXPECTED OUTPUT

You should be able to deliver multiple finalized edits and variations per week, execute fast revisions based on performance data, and maintain clean organized project files.

YOU HAVE TO HAVE EXPERIENCE WITH:

Using AI softwares to create AI avatars, AI b-roll clips, AI voiceovers and many more. You have to be very familiar with AI and interested in learning more about it and how to integrated more into your work.

TOOLS

We use CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or equivalent. Notion for task management and communication.

IN ADDITION to base pay, you will receive performance-based bonuses on winning Meta ads.

IMPORTANT

Please apply only if you have at least 2 years of relevant experience in video editing for paid ads or performance content. Include samples of ad creatives, UGC edits, or Meta video ads in your application. No portfolio = no review.

title: Social Media Designer (Shorts Creator)
description:
Social Media Designer (Shorts Creator)

As It Is In Heaven (AiiiH)



Application Procedure

Applicants who do not strictly follow these instructions will be automatically disqualified. Please submit at this URL - 
 – a customized resume that begins with the following two sections:

1. Portfolio 

o Social Media Short 1 (include hyperlink) 

o Social Media Short 2 (include hyperlink) 

o Social Media Short 3 (include hyperlink) 

2. Cover Letter 

o Include a 2–3 paragraph cover letter explaining why you are interested in this position and what sets you apart from other candidates.



About Us

As It Is In Heaven is a Christian Catholic ministry committed to bringing Heaven to Earth through worship, deliverance, and healing. Our vision is to see Christians around the world saved, healed, and delivered, and then equipped to walk in the power and authority of Jesus Christ.



Why Join Us

• Your work directly contributes to spreading the Gospel of Jesus Christ and watching people be healed, delivered, and saved globally.

• Be mentored by leaders actively walking in Christ’s power and authority

• Join a movement at a pivotal growth 
nt—not just a ministry

• As the ministry scales, there will be ongoing career growth opportunities for you like a Lead Content Creator, Social Media Manager, or Marketing Manager



Requirements

• Strong alignment with and passion for the mission of As It Is In Heaven

• Proven ability to create viral or high-performing short-form content (especially on
 and YouTube)

• Must have skills using photo and video editing apps like Canva, Opus Clips, and VEED 

• Portfolio of past work for brands or personal channels

• Strong written and spoken English communication skills

• Proficiency in AI tools to streamline content creation and publishing

• Ability to create engaging, humorous, and witty content when appropriate

• Ability to maintain high attention to detail in a fast-paced environment  



Preferred Qualifications

• Bachelor’s degree (or in progress) in Marketing, Communications, or a related field



Key Responsibilities

• As a Social Media Shorts Creator, you will be assigned two social media channels 
 and 
) and will be expected to increase membership, post content, and respond to inquiries as the voice of the channel - engaging and building community.

• Create short-form video content using clips from OpusClips and VEED accounts

• Stay current on short-form content trends and position AiiiH as a leader in the space

• Track social media engagement to identify high-performing ideas and campaigns for scalability.  

• Optimize content for engagement, reach, and platform-specific performance

• Collaborate with leadership to pay to promote high-performing videos (“pour gas on the fire”)

• While weekly prayer calls provide source material, creators are expected to enhance content through original contributions (e.g., introductions, reactions, “watch-alongs,” or commentary)

o Be willing to appear on camera when needed—not relying solely on AiiiH leaders for content

• Report performance and insights in 
 (AiiiH’s ERP system) to track trends and drive growth

• Meet with your manager on a weekly basis to follow through on the 90 day plan below



What Success Looks Like (First 45 Days)



First 14 Days



• Audit existing content and identify patterns in top-performing videos

• Fully understand AiiiH’s voice, theology, and target audience

• Begin publishing consistently (minimum agreed cadence)

• Launch initial test content across
 and YouTube Shorts



Days 14-30



• Identify 2–3 repeatable content formats that show strong engagement

• Demonstrate increasing consistency in views, retention, and shares

• Begin incorporating original on-camera content (hooks, reactions, commentary)

• Build and share a clear performance dashboard with actionable insights



Days 30-45



• Produce at least 1–2 videos with breakout performance (viral or near-viral traction)

• Establish a scalable content system using AI and workflows

• Clearly demonstrate ability to “double down” on winning content

• Contribute to measurable growth in followers, engagement, and reach

• Operate with increasing ownership—bringing ideas, not just execution



Compensation & Performance Incentives

• Competitive hourly rate (Philippines-based), commensurate with experience and performance



Performance-based bonuses tied to content outcomes, such as:

• Hitting defined view thresholds (e.g., 50K, 100K, 250K+ views)

• High engagement metrics (shares, saves, comments)

• Viral content performance



Growth-based incentives:

• Bonuses tied to follower growth and channel expansion

• Opportunities for increased pay based on consistent performance



Long-term opportunity:

• Potential to grow into a Lead Content Creator, Social Media Manager, or Marketing Manager role as we launch a podcast and responsibilities increase

• Increased ownership and compensation as the ministry scales

title: Experienced Associate Brand Manager to support 12+ Amazon brands for growth, execution, drive performance and operation
description:
Role Overview



We're hiring 2 Associate Brand Manager who will work directly under the Senior Brand Manager to execute day-to-day brand operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the “legwork” that drives performance: optimizing listings, managing keyword research, coordinating creative projects, and pulling actionable data.



If the Senior Brand Manager sets the playbook, the Associate Brand Manager runs the plays — ensuring everything gets executed on time, correctly, and at a high level.



Key Responsibilities

1. Listing Management & Optimization

Create, update, and fully optimize Amazon product listings (titles, bullets, descriptions, images, videos, A+ content).

Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis to improve rankings and visibility.

Ensure all listings remain Rufus and Cosmos compliant with Amazon’s latest requirements.

Test content variations (A/B testing) to improve CTR and conversions.



2. Data Research & Analysis

Pull performance data from Amazon Seller Central and external tools (Data Dive, Helium 10, Jungle Scout).

Identify trends, keyword shifts, and competitor activity.

Provide actionable recommendations to the Senior Brand Manager to improve conversions, CTR, and sales velocity.



3. Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with the graphic design team to request, coordinate, and deliver images, infographics, and A+ creatives.

Partner with the marketing team to support product launches, promotions, and review-generation initiatives.

Ensure all projects are aligned with deadlines, strategy, and brand guidelines.



4. Conversion-Focused Execution

Continuously update and refine listings to improve conversion rates and customer engagement.

Maintain Amazon Brand Stores, A+ content, and ad creatives for consistency and performance.

Assist in preparing assets for influencer campaigns and external marketing pushes.



5. Project & Compliance Support

Own the administrative “heavy lifting” of flat files, bulk uploads, and catalog troubleshooting.

Flag compliance issues, unauthorized sellers, or listing errors for quick resolution.

Support market expansion efforts by preparing research and draft listings for new product opportunities.



Skills & Qualifications



2+ years experience in Amazon FBA operations, brand management, or digital marketing.

Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, flat files, listing creation, and troubleshooting.

Strong skills in SEO, keyword research, and listing optimization.

Data-driven mindset; confident in Google Sheets and reporting.

Strong communication skills to work cross-functionally with design, marketing, and operations teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.



What Success Looks Like

Every listing across assigned brands is fully optimized, compliant, and performing.

Senior Brand Manager receives clear, accurate reports and recommendations.

Creative assets are delivered on time because you coordinated smoothly with design.



Product launches happen seamlessly with marketing and review strategies in place.

Conversions and rankings consistently improve due to your execution. If you feel you are the right fit, please respond to the job with the title " The Best Supporting Branding Manager Out There!"



Please respond directly to :

----------



Kind Regards,

Tam Ma

Co Founder

Prodigy Media

title: Patient Care Coordinator
description:
We are looking for a highly motivated and experienced Remote Patient Services Representative based in the Philippines. The ideal candidate will manage inbound and outbound calls, assist patients with scheduling appointments, address inquiries, and perform other related tasks to ensure a seamless patient experience.



Key Responsibilities:

• Answer incoming calls promptly and professionally.

• Schedule, reschedule, and confirm patient appointments using our electronic health record (EHR) system.

• Provide accurate information regarding services, insurance, and payment options.

• Address patient inquiries and concerns with empathy and efficiency.

• Maintain and update patient records with a high degree of accuracy.

• Collaborate with the optometry team to ensure optimal patient care.



Performance Metrics:

To ensure excellence in patient service, the following performance metrics will be used to evaluate success in this role:

• Average Handle Time (AHT): The average duration of a patient interaction, including talk time, hold time, and after-call work. Efficiently managing AHT ensures timely assistance to patients while maintaining service quality. ?

• Quality Score: Assessment of call quality based on adherence to protocols, accuracy of information provided, and overall patient interaction effectiveness. High-quality scores reflect a commitment to excellence in patient care.

• Patient Satisfaction Score: A measure of patient contentment with the service provided, often gathered through post-interaction surveys. High scores indicate successful patient engagement and issue resolution.

• Service Level: The percentage of calls answered within a specified timeframe, reflecting the ability to meet patient demand promptly. Maintaining a high service level ensures patient satisfaction and loyalty. ?

• Appointment Scheduling Rate: The efficiency and accuracy in scheduling patient appointments, ensuring optimal utilization of clinic time and resources.



Qualifications:

• Proven experience in a customer service role, preferably in a medical or optometry setting.

• Exceptional verbal and written English communication skills.

• Familiarity with EHR systems and medical scheduling software is a plus.

• Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

• Ability to work independently and manage time effectively.

• Stable internet connection and a quiet workspace conducive to professional phone conversations.



Work Schedule:

• Full-time position.

• Must be available to work U.S. business hours, including potential evening shifts to accommodate different time zones.



To Qualify, please complete the Assessment below:



1. Application form: wkf.ms/45nfpCJ

2. Complete this assessment: 
/link/index/JB-BYUGHNDRC?u=

title: Patient Care Coordinator
description:
Job Title: Bookkeeper (US-Based Operations)



We are seeking a sharp, proactive, and highly experienced Bookkeeper to take full ownership of our financial records. This isn't just a data entry role; we need a "numbers architect" who catches what others miss, operates with total autonomy, and treats our ledger like a high-stakes puzzle they are determined to solve.



If you are a QuickBooks expert who thrives in a fast-paced environment and prides yourself on presenting insights before they are even requested, you’re exactly who we’re looking for.



Key Responsibilities



Full-Cycle Bookkeeping: Complete oversight of QuickBooks, including sophisticated AP/AR management, complex journal entries, and meticulous ledger maintenance.

Proactive Reporting: Prepare and present monthly P&L, Balance Sheets, and Cash Flow statements with executive-level clarity.

Process Optimization: Audit current workflows to identify "bottlenecks" and implement streamlined, automated processes for maximum efficiency.

Advanced Reconciliation: Handle high-volume bank and credit card reconciliations with 100?curacy, investigating and resolving discrepancies independently.

US Compliance: Manage financial records in accordance with US standards, ensuring readiness for year-end tax preparation and audits.

Platform Integration: Quickly master and integrate secondary platforms (payroll, time-tracking, or inventory) with QuickBooks.



What We’re Looking For:



Experience: Proven track record (3+ years preferred) as a Bookkeeper or Accountant, specifically working with US-based companies.

QuickBooks Expert: You are a power user. You know the shortcuts, the advanced reporting features, and how to troubleshoot the software's quirks.

High Autonomy: You don't need a task list. You see what needs to be done, you do it, and you report the results.

Analytical Mindset: You don’t just move numbers; you understand what they mean for the business and can explain "the why" to leadership.



Tech-Agile: You learn new software at lightning speed and are always looking for tools to make bookkeeping faster and more accurate.



To Qualify – Complete the Assessment and Application Form:

1. Assessment: 
/o/JB-E0PIHSF1P/landing?u=
. Application Form: wkf.ms/45nfpCJ



Note: Both the assessment and the application form must be completed to be considered for this role.

As an Appointment Setter, your #1 responsibility is to call and text leads the 
nt they opt in, engage them in a professional, friendly conversation, and book them for an appointment. You’ll be the first point of contact and play a key role in helping people take the first step in their health journey.

 leads using our CRM to increase show-up rates.

We are able to do this by providing testing and treatments that are inaccessible through conventional medicine so you can ultimately….. prevent disease and live as healthy as possible… is that what you're looking for or are you looking for something else?"





How much does it cost?: "That's a really good question, I am only in charge of scheduling, I really would love to help you and the thing is we have a wide range of testing and treatments so there really isn't a way for me to tell you what this is going to cost. But what I can do is set you up with someone who is trained and can go over your medical history and see how we can actually help. I have time [next available appointment] with a patient coordinator, does that work for you?'

 A booked prospect who attends their discovery call and pays for the functional medicine program

title: YouTube Video Editor – Long Interview ? Clips, Shorts & Full Edit (URGENT)
description:
YouTube Video Editor – Long Interview ? Clips, Shorts & Full Edit (URGENT)



**Job Description:**

I’m looking for a skilled video editor to transform a 1 hour 54 minute interview (MP4 file) into a full YouTube video, mini episodes, and short-form content for social media.



This project also includes removing a translator from the video and cleaning up the audio so the final result feels natural and professional.



I need someone who understands pacing, storytelling, and how to create engaging content across platforms.



---



**Scope of Work:**



**1. Full-Length Video**



* Edit the full interview for YouTube

* Clean cuts, remove unnecessary pauses

* Remove translator visually where possible

* Clean up or replace translator audio

* Add basic polish (audio leveling, color correction, etc.)

* Add timestamps/chapters (if possible)



**2. Mini Episodes (YouTube Clips)**



* Break the interview into **10–15 strong segments**

* Each clip should focus on a clear topic or takeaway

* Length: ~5–12 minutes each

* Optimized for engagement and retention



**3. Short-Form Content**



* Create **20–40 short clips** for:



  * TikTok

  * Instagram (Reels)

  * YouTube Shorts

* Length: 20–60 seconds

* Vertical format (9:16)

* Add captions (bold, engaging style)

* Use jump cuts / zooms to keep attention high

* Strong hook in first 1–2 seconds



---



**Key Requirements:**



* Proven experience editing YouTube interviews or podcast-style content

* Strong storytelling instincts (knows what to cut and what to keep)

* Audio editing skills (cleanup, voice adjustment/replacement if needed)

* Ability to remove or crop out a third person (translator) cleanly

* Familiar with short-form content trends (TikTok/Reels/Shorts)



---



**Bonus Skills (Preferred):**



* Experience with AI voice tools or dubbing

* Motion graphics for subtle transitions

* Thumbnail suggestions or packaging insight



---



**Deliverables:**



* 1 full edited interview (YouTube-ready)

* 10–15 mini episode clips

* 20–40 short-form videos (vertical)

* Project files (optional but preferred)



---



**Timeline:**

ASAP – please include your estimated turnaround time.



---



**To Apply:**

Please include:



* Links to similar work (especially interviews, podcasts, or short-form clips)

* A brief explanation of how you would approach this project

* Your rate (fixed or hourly)



---



Looking for someone reliable who can deliver high-quality work quickly. Potential for ongoing work if this goes well.

title: Estimator / Quantity Surveyor
description:
Overview



We are seeking a skilled and detail-oriented Quantity Surveyor / Estimator to join our construction team in a fully remote capacity. The successful candidate will be responsible for preparing accurate cost estimates, managing project budgets, and supporting the delivery of construction projects. 



This role is ideal for a self-motivated professional who can work independently, manage multiple projects, and maintain strong communication in a remote working environment.



Key Responsibilities



Prepare detailed cost estimates and tender submissions for construction projects.

Review drawings, specifications, and project documentation remotely to determine scope and cost requirements.

Conduct quantity take-offs and prepare bills of quantities using digital tools.

Analyse subcontractor and supplier quotations to ensure competitiveness and accuracy.

Assist with procurement of subcontractors and materials.

Monitor project costs and track variations throughout the project lifecycle.

Prepare and manage project budgets, forecasts, and cost reports.

Assess and manage contractual claims and variations.

Liaise with project managers, engineers, clients, and subcontractors via online platforms (e.g., Teams, Zoom,
).

Identify cost-saving opportunities and value engineering options.

Maintain accurate digital records and documentation.

Ensure compliance with contractual terms, regulations, and company procedures.



Required Qualifications and Experience



Degree or equivalent qualification in Quantity Surveying, Construction Management, or a related field.

Proven experience in quantity surveying and/or estimating within the construction industry.

Strong knowledge of construction contracts, cost control, and procurement processes.

Ability to interpret construction drawings and technical specifications.

Proficiency in estimating software and Microsoft Office applications.

Experience working remotely or managing projects independently is highly desirable.

Strong numerical, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Ability to manage time effectively and meet deadlines without direct supervision.

==

title: Technical Delivery Manager
description:
We're looking for a Technical Delivery Manager to own the end-to-end delivery of our client integration projects.



You'll be as comfortable reviewing an technical specifications as you are presenting a project status to a steering committee. You'll run the sprints, manage the risks, keep the client informed, and make sure the team has everything they need to deliver great work.



You'll take full ownership of project outcomes - scope, timeline, budget, quality. When things go sideways (and they will), you'll be the one who stays calm, works the problem, and gets things back on track.



What You'll Do



- Run projects end-to-end: from kickoff through go-live and hypercare, across systems integration engagements (primarily MuleSoft)

- Lead Agile delivery: facilitate daily stand-ups, sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives

- Own the client relationship: deliver status reports, present to steering committees, and manage expectations proactively

- Get into the technical details: review designs, understand integration architecture, and have credible conversations with both developers and client technical leads

- Manage risk: maintain RAID logs, surface issues early, and always come with a recommendation

- Plan capacity: forecast resourcing across projects and coordinate with leadership on team allocation

- Remove blockers: shield the delivery team from noise and make sure they can focus on building



What We're Looking For



- 5+ years delivering IT projects in a consulting or professional services environment

- Bachelor's degree (engineering background a plus)

- Solid understanding of systems integration patterns — APIs, messaging, ETL, event-driven architecture

- Scrum certification or demonstrable Agile delivery experience

- Experience managing project budgets, timelines, and resource allocation

- Excellent written and verbal English communication

- Comfortable presenting to senior stakeholders, including C-suite and steering committees



Why Performent Consulting



- Real ownership: you won't be a cog in a machine. You'll own your projects and have a direct impact on client outcomes and company growth.

- Technical depth: we're a consulting firm that cares about engineering quality, not just billable hours.

- Growth trajectory: we're scaling, and this role has a clear path to Head of Delivery, Practice Lead, or Client Partner.

- Flexible and remote: work from anywhere in the APAC region. We trust our people to manage their time.

- Small team, big clients: enterprise-grade work without the enterprise-grade bureaucracy.



To Apply

If you are someone who takes extreme ownership and loves to ensure that the client is happy and the team can perform to their highest potential, we'd love to hear from you. Please apply on the OnlineJobs platform AND fill in this application form:

title: YouTube Video Editor | $2,500/mo | Full-Time, Long-Term
description:
YouTube Video Editor | $2,500/mo | Full-Time, Long-Term



Full-Time YouTube Video Editor, Business Finance Channel (Long-Form + Shorts)

$2,500/month | Full-Time | Long-Term



ABOUT THE CHANNEL

We're building one of the best business finance channels on YouTube. The content helps business owners understand their numbers: how to increase revenue, improve profit margins, and most importantly, manage cash flow. The audience is business owners who are hungry to grow and want someone to explain the financial side in a way that actually makes sense.



This is not content for accountants. This is content for operators. People running businesses who need to understand their money the same way they understand their product. The editing needs to match that energy. Clean, yes. Calm, yes. But never boring. Every video should feel like the viewer is getting an unfair advantage by watching it.



We're looking for ONE editor to own the production side of this channel and grow with it long-term. This is not a freelance gig with occasional videos. This is a full-time role with consistent weekly output, a production system designed to set you up for success, and pay that reflects the level of work we're asking for.



HOW WE THINK ABOUT CONTENT

We study channels like Alex Hormozi, Codie Sanchez, and Graham Stephan. Not to copy their style, but to understand why their content performs. What we've learned is that the best business content on YouTube does three things:



1. It hooks immediately. The first 5 seconds determine whether someone stays or scrolls. The opening frame, the first line of text on screen, the pacing of the first cut. All of it matters. We want an editor who thinks about this on every single video.



2. It keeps people watching. A talking head can hold attention for hours if the editing creates rhythm. That means knowing when to cut to B-roll, when to drop a graphic that reframes what was just said, when to let a beat of silence land, and when to tighten a section because the energy is dipping. Retention is not the creator's job alone. It's a partnership between what's said and how it's edited.



3. It earns the click. Thumbnails aren't an afterthought. They're the front door. We want someone who studies what makes people click on business content and brings that thinking to every thumbnail.



We're not making hype content. We're not making guru content. But we are making content that competes with the best channels in the space, and that requires an editor who understands YouTube as a platform, not just a place to upload videos.



THE PRODUCTION SYSTEM WE'VE BUILT FOR YOU

One of the biggest frustrations editors face is guessing what the creator wants. We've eliminated that. Every single video comes with a complete production package so you can focus on what you do best: editing. Here's what you'll receive for every long-form video:



- Raw footage (1080p, one camera angle)

- A full script with notes in [brackets] marking exactly where to place every B-roll clip, graphic, music change, and screen recording. No guessing, no interpretation needed.

- Screen recordings ready to drop in

- Stock footage search terms with Storyblocks account access

- Background music direction with Epidemic Sound account access

- Thumbnail direction with layout and text specified



Your job is never to wonder what goes where. Your job is to make it look, sound, and feel like content people can't stop watching.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN

You will be responsible for all video production on the channel. To start, that means:



- 3 polished long-form YouTube videos per week (typically 10 to 15 minutes each, talking head with B-roll)

- 8 to 10 short-form clips per week (Shorts/Reels repurposed from long-form content)

- 1 thumbnail per long-form video



As we build out our B-roll library, templates, and workflow together, we will scale to 5 to 6+ long-form videos per week. The goal is to grow into that together, not to sprint into it on day one.



WHAT A GREAT LONG-FORM EDIT LOOKS LIKE

- The first 5 seconds are engineered. The opening hook, whether it's a bold statement, a surprising number, or a pattern interrupt, is paced and cut to grab attention immediately. If the opening feels slow, it gets tightened before we ever see it.



- Bad takes, pauses, and filler words are removed. The viewer never feels a rough spot.



- The pacing creates rhythm. A talking-head video should never feel like one long, unbroken monologue. The edit creates natural breathing room through B-roll, graphics, and visual breaks. Not because the script says so, but because the editor feels where the energy needs a shift. If there's more than 30 seconds of straight talking head, something gets inserted.



- Audio is clean: background noise removed, voice levels consistent throughout, no jarring volume shifts between sections.



- Color correction is light and natural. The viewer sees a clean, well-lit image without noticing the correction.



- Stock B-roll appears at every marked point, clips are 2 to 4 seconds each, and they actually match the topic being discussed. If the script says "insert B-roll of warehouse operations" and the clip shows a generic office, that gets swapped before we ever review it.



- On-screen text does real work. Text and graphics aren't decoration. They reinforce the point being made. When we compare "$6 product" vs "$18 shipping," that graphic should land at exactly the right 
nt to make the viewer think "wow, I never saw it that way." Clean white text on a dark bar or semi-transparent background, appearing one line at a time, formatted consistently across every video.



- Screen recordings are dropped in at marked points with smooth transitions.



- One background music track runs through the video at roughly 10 to 15% of voice volume. Low, no lyrics, documentary feel, with volume shifts at marked points.



- Clean captions/subtitles run the full video: white text, dark outline, no bouncing or animation.



- The YouTube export is 1080p, high bitrate, uploaded to Google Drive.



WHAT A GREAT SHORT-FORM EDIT LOOKS LIKE

- The strongest 30 to 60 second 
nts are pulled from each long-form video. Not random clips, but 
nts that stand alone and make someone want to see more.



- The hook is everything. The first 2 seconds determine whether someone watches or scrolls. If the opening doesn't grab attention, the clip gets re-cut or a different 
nt gets chosen. Over time, you develop an instinct for which 
nts will stop the scroll.



- The clip is reformatted to vertical (9:16) for YouTube Shorts, Instagram Reels, and TikTok.



- Captions are optimized for mobile viewing.



- The same clean, professional style carries through. No flashy effects, no trend-chasing that clashes with the brand.



STYLE RULES: WHAT WE DO AND WHAT WE DON'T

What we don't do:



- No flashy transitions. Simple cuts only.

- No zoom effects or artificial camera movement.

- No sound effects, whooshes, or stingers.

- No trend-chasing edits that feel out of place on a business finance channel.



What we do:



- We use pacing to hold attention. Cuts are deliberate. Sections that drag get tightened. 
nts that need space get it. The edit should feel like a conversation, not a lecture.



- We use graphics to drive points home. Every on-screen element earns its place by making the content land harder. A well-timed number comparison, a key phrase that appears right as it's spoken, a visual that reframes the idea.



- We use B-roll to create visual rhythm. Not just to cover jump cuts, but to give the viewer's eyes something that reinforces the story being told.



- We make thumbnails that earn clicks. Bold, clear, and designed to stand out in a feed full of business content. Every thumbnail should make a business owner think "I need to watch this."



- The overall feel: clean, calm, confident. A channel that looks like it's run by professionals who know exactly what they're doing.



WHAT WINNING LOOKS LIKE IN THIS ROLE

This is specific, because vague expectations help no one:



- Rough cuts land within 24 hours of receiving footage. Not 24 hours after you start. 24 hours after the production package hits your inbox.



- First drafts need minimal notes. A great week is one where our revision rounds are small tweaks, not structural re-edits. That means you watched the final product before sending it and caught the things we would have caught.



- B-roll actually matches. When the script says "insert B-roll of a small business owner reviewing financial statements," you don't drop in a generic stock clip of someone typing on a laptop. You search until the visual matches the idea.



- Audio is invisible. The viewer never notices the audio because it's perfect. No sudden volume jumps between sections, no background hum that crept through, no music that overpowers the voice.



- The first 5 seconds of every video are sharp. You don't just edit the video. You engineer the opening. If the hook doesn't hit, you flag it or re-cut it before sending the draft.



- Shorts get views. You pay attention to which clips perform and which don't. Over time, you develop a feel for what makes a strong Short from our content, and you pick those 
nts without being told.



- Thumbnails get clicks. You track which thumbnails perform and start recognizing patterns. You bring that knowledge into every new thumbnail you create.



- You flag problems before they become problems. If footage is unusable, if a screen recording is missing, if something in the script doesn't make sense, you tell us immediately instead of waiting until the deadline.



- The channel looks like one person edited every video. Consistency across every thumbnail, every caption style, every graphic format. A viewer should never be able to tell which video was edited on a Monday versus a Friday.



- You care about the numbers. Not because we're going to micromanage analytics, but because you want to know if viewers are dropping off at the 3-minute mark and whether the edit might be part of the reason. An editor who studies retention data becomes an exponentially better editor over time.



WHERE THIS ROLE GOES

This isn't a job where the ceiling is "edit videos forever at the same level." Here's what growth looks like:



- Months 1 to 3: Learn the system, build speed, establish consistency. We build our B-roll library and template files together. By month 3, the workflow should feel automatic.



- Months 4 to 6: You start having real input on content. Which thumbnails are driving clicks? Which Shorts are performing? What editing patterns keep viewers watching longer? Your observations start shaping how we produce videos.



- Months 6 to 12: As we scale to 5 to 6+ long-form videos per week, you may help onboard and train a second editor. You become the person who owns the production standard for the channel. The one who knows how everything should look and sound.



- Beyond year one: The right person in this role becomes a true creative partner. Someone whose judgment on what works is trusted because they've earned it through hundreds of videos. We're building a channel that competes with the biggest names in business content. The editor who helps us get there will have built something they're proud of.



We invest in people who invest in this work.



SOFTWARE YOU NEED



- DaVinci Resolve, Premiere Pro, or Final Cut Pro

- Canva or Photoshop (for thumbnails)

- CapCut or similar tool for short-form editing

- Stable, fast internet for uploading/downloading large files daily



TO APPLY

1. Send a link to ONE long-form YouTube video you edited (10+ minutes, talking head with B-roll)

2. Send a link to ONE short-form clip you edited (YouTube Short, Reel, or TikTok)

3. Tell us which editing software you use

4. In 2 to 3 sentences, tell us what you'd do differently if you edited that long-form video today

5. Look at the first 30 seconds of your sample video. Does the opening hook land? Tell us why or why not. (This question matters more than you think.)

6. Tell us why you want a full-time editing role, not freelance project work



Applications are reviewed personally and strong candidates will hear back quickly.
