We will review your submission and reply as soon as possible.

title: Paid Social & Performance Content Specialist
description:
Role Overview

The Paid Social & Performance Content Specialist creates, adapts and deploys high-quality creative that performs across TikTok Paid, Meta Paid, YouTube, Google (Demand Gen/YouTube/Display), Yelp 1P, DTC,
/SMS and third-party marketplaces.

We’re looking for a self-governing, fast, commercially minded creator who understands that creative is a core performance lever. You should be comfortable operating with minimal direction while staying aligned to brand guardrails, campaign priorities and performance feedback.

AI is central to the workflow, but human judgment, brand sensibility and final creative control always sit on top. This role sits inside the paid performance engine, where speed, consistency and quality matter as much as ideas.



What You’ll Do

Paid Strategy & Planning

Support execution of performance-driven paid creative strategy.

Maintain weekly/monthly creative calendars aligned to campaigns and launches.

Translate briefs from Paid Media, Ecommerce and Marketing into platform-ready creative for TikTok, Meta, YouTube, Google, Yelp 1P, DTC,
/SMS and marketplaces.

Propose creative angles, formats and tests based on performance trends.

Paid Creative Production

Produce platform-native assets including:

Short-form vertical video (TikTok, Reels, Shorts)

Mid-form YouTube creative

Static images and carousels

UGC-style performance video

Write conversion-oriented hooks, captions and CTAs.

Optimize creative for pacing, framing, text density and platform best practices.

Ensure all work meets brand standards while feeling native to each channel.

AI-Powered Workflow

Use AI tools for hook generation, A/B testing, video editing, thumbnail/caption variants and rapid iteration at scale.

Balance efficiency with brand integrity, treating AI as a production amplifier, not the decision maker.

Publishing, QA & Alignment

Prepare assets to exact specs for Meta, TikTok, YouTube, Google, Yelp and marketplaces.

Partner closely with Paid Media teams/agencies for smooth implementation.

Maintain organized asset libraries and clear naming conventions.

Ecommerce & Marketplace Integration

Design creative that supports conversion and product discovery.

Align timing and messaging with DTC promotions, paid campaigns,
/SMS and marketplace initiatives.

Ensure marketplace assets meet platform requirements while preserving brand consistency.

Performance & Learning

Review results including CTR, CPC, CPA, CPV, hook rate, watch time and view-through signals.

Refine storytelling, structure and hooks based on data.

Share concise creative learnings with stakeholders.

Collaboration & Trends

Work cross-functionally with Paid Media, Ecommerce, Marketing and Creative teams.

Stay current on platform updates and ad best practices across Meta, TikTok, YouTube, Google and Yelp.



You Bring

2–5 years experience in paid social creative, performance content, or ecommerce marketing.

Proven track record of high-performing short-form video and static paid assets.

Experience working with paid teams or agencies preferred.

Familiarity with YouTube and Google ad formats a strong plus.

Comfort owning workflows and deliverables with minimal supervision.

Working knowledge of CTR, CPC, CPA, CPV and hook rate.

Highly organized with strong attention to detail.

A portfolio demonstrating TikTok, Meta, YouTube and ecommerce-oriented paid creative is required.

Tools

Native ad tools across Meta, TikTok, Google, YouTube and Yelp.

Canva, CapCut, Adobe Creative Suite, or equivalent.

Hands-on experience with AI creative tools for variation and testing.

Ability to build scalable, repeatable creative workflows.

What Success Looks Like

Consistent delivery of high-quality, campaign-ready creative.

Reliable, on-time handoffs to Paid Media teams.

Improving creative efficiency and performance over time.

Clear contribution to paid-driven traffic and revenue across TikTok, Meta, YouTube, Google and Yelp 1P.

title: Junior UI/UX & Graphic Designer — Figma + Adobe Suite (Subcontractor)
description:
I'm a Canadian web design agency owner looking for a Junior UI/UX & Graphic Designer to join me as a long-term subcontractor. I handle the client relationships and set the creative direction — you help me execute and build on it.

This is a growth role. I'm not looking for someone who has done everything already. I'm looking for someone with solid Figma fundamentals, a good eye, and the drive to learn fast. I will teach you how I work, how I think about design systems, and how to prepare files that developers can actually build from.



Schedule



6 hours per day, Monday to Friday (30 hrs/week)

EST overlap required — you need to be available during Canadian business hours for part of your day

3–4 short Zoom sessions per week for brainstorming, design reviews, and direction (30–60 min each)

Outside of meetings, you work independently at your own pace



What you'll do



Work in Figma to extend and maintain design systems based on brand directions I provide

Design website mockups (desktop + mobile) ready for developer handoff — clean, organized, annotated

Create graphic design assets: brochures, stationery, social media graphics, presentations, and other print/digital materials

Organize and prepare Figma files for developers (components, variants, spacing, specs)

Handle image editing, cropping, and optimization in Adobe Photoshop or Affinity Photo

Create vector graphics, icons, and illustrations in Adobe Illustrator or Affinity Designer

Work in Canva when client deliverables call for it

Participate actively in design reviews — ask questions, flag issues, give input



What you need



Figma — you must know auto layout, components, and basic prototyping. Design systems experience is a big plus, but if you understand the concept and want to learn, that counts

Adobe Suite (Photoshop + Illustrator + InDesign) OR Affinity Designer/Publisher/Photo — at least two of these confidently

Canva — basic working knowledge

A genuine eye for design — not just execution ability, but aesthetic judgment

Strong written English — you'll communicate with me daily via chat and occasionally explain your decisions

Reliable internet and a computer that can handle Figma + Adobe without freezing

Willingness to follow direction, ask when unsure, and iterate without ego







What this is

This is an independent subcontractor arrangement, not employment. You invoice monthly. You control your schedule outside of our agreed meeting times and overlap window. I'm looking for someone to build with long-term — if you're good and reliable, hours and rate grow with the relationship.



To apply

Send your portfolio or Figma file samples. If you don't have a portfolio link, attach work samples directly. Applications without design work will not be reviewed.

title: Seeking: Marketing, Strategy & Social Media Content Creator for Conscious Healing Brand
description:
Role: Marketing, Strategy & Social Media for Tantric/Healing Practice Hours: 10–15 hours per week Rate: $7.50 per hour Location: Remote



About the role



I'm a tantric and shamanic practitioner and activist who bridges personal and collective healing with action for social and environmental justice. I need someone skilled and self-directed to help me grow my tantric practice into a financially sustainable business.



This is an employed role, but I'm looking for someone who wants to think and develop with me — not just execute tasks. I want to work with someone who brings their own ideas, takes initiative, and cares about doing meaningful work.



What you'd be doing:



* Marketing & social media strategy — content planning, audience building, and positioning in the wellness/activist/tantra/healing/social movement space

* Social media execution — repurposing my teachings, writings, audio, and video into posts; editing and scheduling content; managing social media advertising

* Market research — understanding the landscape and identifying opportunities

* Website management — content updates and basic site maintenance (
)

* Ticket sales support — helping to fill workshops and retreats through effective campaigns?

What I'm offering

* $7.50/hour, 10–15 hours per week

* Creative freedom and genuine input into how the business grows

* Work in the growing tantric/healing/social justice space

* A collaborative working relationship with someone who values your thinking



What I'm looking for:



* Experience in digital marketing, social media strategy and content creation — ideally in some of the following spaces: wellness, healing, spiritual, coaching, social and environmental justice in London and Amsterdam 

* Ability to work independently, bring ideas, and manage your own time

* Familiarity with social media advertising and scheduling tools

* Basic skills in content creation and editing (image, audio, written, video) 



We would work together to create meaningfully content, build a following and sell tickets for upcoming events. 



Who this might be a good fit for



Ideally, I’m looking for someone who understands both the spiritual and healing market and the social justice  and NGO space — who can help grow this into something sustainable.



I would like to work with someone who cares about the intersection of spirituality, healing, and social justice — and, like me, believes that both communities can benefit by being influenced by each other 



If you're interested in helping to create experiences that support real transformation for individuals, communities and society more generally — I'd love to hear from you.



About my work



My somatic approaches support people to recognise the individual, social, and historical roots of their trauma and release blocks from the body that hold both themselves and society back. My tantric works creates spaces where people can learn to use life-energy to enter healing states of consciousness where a felt understanding of the interconnected nature of reality is possible and waken there innate abilities to connect to their intuition. These practices help participants break through personal limitations, co-create with others from an assumption of interconnectedness and relationality and encourages engagement with broader social transformation.



My political work supports teams and organisations to centre decoloniality and somatic understanding in their design and decision-making. By embracing understandings of indigenous peoples, complex systems, and somatic, tantric and shamanic practices, I facilitate organisational innovations and cultural transformations for those interested in working towards systemic change.?

It is my intention to bring these two strands of my work closer together.



Learn more about my tantric work: 
 

Learn more about my activist work: 



To apply:



Send me a message with:

* A little about yourself and why this interests you

* Your relevant experience (links to past work or social media accounts you've managed are helpful)?

title: Senior Project Manager — JLC Contracting | U.S. Construction
description:
Senior Project Manager — JLC Contracting | U.S. Construction

Division 9 | Interior Fit-Outs | GC & Subcontract Work | Public Works & Federal Work

Remote (Philippines) | $6–$12/hr Based on Experience | Negotiable for the Right Candidate | Full-time



Read This First



This is not an entry-level PM role. This is not for someone still learning the industry. This is not for someone who needs to be walked through a set of drawings.



We are looking for a Senior Project Manager with 10+ years of hands-on U.S. construction experience — someone who has managed complex projects from bid to closeout, knows Division 9 work cold, can read a full set of construction documents without hesitation, and can run multiple projects simultaneously without dropping a single ball.



You will work directly with Executives and entire project team of JLC Contracting and interface daily with GC project teams, Owners, Owner's Reps, Architects, Engineers, and our internal field crews.



This role carries real responsibility. We expect senior-level output from day one.



If you do not have 10+ years of direct U.S. construction PM experience — please do not apply.



About JLC Contracting



JLC Contracting is a U.S.-based construction company operating as both a specialty subcontractor and general contractor/CM depending on project type.



As a Subcontractor:

- We self-perform all Division 9 work — flooring, ceilings, wall finishes, specialty interiors

- We bid and execute Division 9 scopes through GCs on commercial, institutional, and high-rise projects

- We respond to public works bids at the municipal, state, and federal level



As a General Contractor:

- We take on high-end interior fit-out projects as the GC

- We manage full project delivery from award through closeout including all trades



Our work spans ground-up high-rise construction, interior fit-outs, corporate and retail refresh programs, and re-branding projects across the U.S.



What You Will Own



Project Management — Full Lifecycle

- Manage multiple active projects simultaneously from award through final closeout

- Serve as the primary point of contact for GC project teams, Owners, Owner's Reps, Architects, and Engineers

- Coordinate directly with internal field crews and foremen — keep the field informed and on schedule

- Anticipate problems before they become issues — bring solutions, not just status updates



Project Scheduling

- Create, maintain, and update detailed project schedules for all active projects

- Identify critical path items and flag scheduling risks early

- Coordinate schedule impacts with GCs, trades, and field crews in real time

- Proficiency in Project Plan 365 or equivalent required



Document Control & Logs

- Own and maintain all project logs — RFIs, RFPs, submittals, change orders, insurance certificates, and correspondence

- Manage the full RFI process — write, track, follow up, and distribute responses

- Manage the submittal log — track submissions, approvals, and resubmittals to keep the project moving

- Maintain change order log — draft COs, track status, ensure nothing falls through

- All documentation organized, current, and accessible at all times



Blueprint & Specification Review

- Read and interpret full construction document sets — architectural, structural, MEP, and spec books

- Identify scope gaps, conflicts, and coordination issues before they hit the field

- Thorough understanding of **CSI coding** — know where Division 9 scope begins and ends

- Apply NAICS coding correctly for bidding, reporting, and compliance purposes



Actual Cost Reporting

- Produce detailed Actual Cost Reports on all active projects

- Track budgeted vs. actual costs at the line item level

- Flag variances early — identify where the project is losing money before it becomes a problem

- Reports must be detailed, accurate, and delivered on a defined cadence



Scope Writing & Contract Administration

- Write detailed, airtight scopes of work for subcontract and GC projects

- Draft and administer subcontracts and vendor agreements

- Ensure all contracts accurately reflect project scope, schedule, and commercial terms

- Review GC subcontract agreements — identify risk, flag issues, recommend revisions



Estimating Support

- Review bid documents, drawings, and specifications for assigned projects

- Prepare and submit competitive bids for Division 9 subcontract work and interior fit-out GC projects

- Write detailed scopes of work as part of the bid package

- Level subcontractor and supplier quotes — identify scope gaps, overlaps, and anomalies

- Utilize PlanSwift or equivalent for quantity takeoffs

- Apply CSI and NAICS coding correctly on all bid submissions



Technology & Software

You must be proficient — not familiar, not learning — in the following or direct equivalents:



- Procore — project management, document control, RFIs, submittals, change orders

- Bluebeam Revu — markup, takeoff, document management

- Fieldwire or equivalent — field coordination and task management

- Project Plan 365 or equivalent — scheduling

- PlanSwift or equivalent — quantity takeoff and estimating

- Google Workspace — Sheets, Docs, Drive — advanced proficiency required

- Microsoft Excel — advanced proficiency required for cost reporting and tracking



Requirements — All Non-Negotiable



10+ Years U.S. Construction Experience — Direct, hands-on PM experience on U.S. projects. Not support, not coordination — ownership. Required, no exceptions.



Division 9 Knowledge — Deep understanding of flooring, ceilings, wall finishes, and specialty interior scopes. You know what self-performing Division 9 work actually means in the field.



Ground-Up & Interior Experience — You have managed both out-of-the-ground construction (high-rise or commercial) and interior fit-out projects. You understand both environments.



Architectural or Engineering Background — Degree or equivalent professional background in Architecture or Engineering. You read construction documents at a professional level.



Blueprint & Specification Proficiency — You read full document sets fluently — architectural, structural, MEP. You catch what others miss.



CSI & NAICS Coding — You understand and correctly apply CSI division coding and NAICS codes in the context of construction bidding, contracting, and compliance.



Procore — Active, hands-on Procore experience. RFIs, submittals, change orders, document control. Not "I've seen it" — you have run projects in it.



Bluebeam Revu — Markup, takeoff, and document management. Daily use level proficiency.



Scheduling — You build and maintain project schedules. You understand critical path. You use Project Plan 365 or a direct equivalent.



Estimating & Takeoff — You have prepared bids, written scopes, and leveled quotes. PlanSwift or equivalent takeoff software required.



Actual Cost Reporting — You have produced detailed project cost reports tracking budget vs. actual at the line item level. You understand job cost accounting in construction.



Scope & Contract Writing — You write detailed, professional scopes of work and have administered construction contracts.



Written English — Professional — Your written English is clear, professional, and requires no editing. You communicate with GCs, Owners, Architects, and Engineers in writing daily.



EST Overlap — Available to work meaningful hours in U.S. Eastern Time. This is not a pure async role — you are coordinating with live U.S. project teams.



The Professional We Are Looking For



- You have seen everything — schedule delays, scope disputes, CO battles, field coordination failures — and you know how to handle all of it

- You are detail obsessed — nothing leaves your desk that isn't accurate and complete

- You are proactive — you identify issues before they become problems and you come with solutions

- You communicate clearly — with the field, with GCs, with owners, and with Robert

- You are commercially aware — you understand that every RFI, every CO, and every scope gap has a dollar value attached to it

- You take full ownership of your projects — if something is wrong, you fix it



How We Work



You will report directly to Executives, Estimating and Project Team.



Communication

- Primary communication via
, Text, Phone

- Urgent items via WhatsApp

- Responsive during agreed U.S. Eastern Time working hours



Daily Rhythm

- Morning check-in to The Team — active project status, issues flagged, priorities for the day

- End of day written update on open items, critical actions, and anything requiring Robert's input

- All project logs and trackers kept current — not weekly, daily



Weekly Review

- Weekly video call with Team — project status, cost reports, schedule updates, open issues

- You come prepared with current data, not summaries fro
mory



Work Assignment

- You manage your projects — Executives do not want to babysit the schedule or the logs

- Issues and decisions requiring principal input are escalated clearly with a recommendation attached



Compensation & Growth



- $8–$15/hr based on proven experience — rate is negotiable for the right candidate. We recognize exceptional experience and we reward it.

- Full-time position with long-term stability

- Performance bonuses tied to project results

- Direct working relationship with company principal

- Growth opportunity as the company scales



How to Apply — Follow These Instructions Exactly



Submit your resume and answer every question below in full. **Any application missing answers to any question will be disqualified immediately. No exceptions.



1. Construction Experience

Describe your construction PM experience — how many years, what project types, what was the largest project you managed by dollar value, and what was your specific role?



2. Division 9 Experience

Describe your specific experience with Division 9 work. Have you worked with a self-performing subcontractor? What scopes have you managed — flooring, ceilings, wall finishes, specialties?



3. Software Proficiency

List every platform from the following that you have used professionally and describe your specific experience with each: Procore, Bluebeam Revu, Fieldwire, Project Plan 365, PlanSwift. For any you have not used, name the equivalent you have used instead.



4. Estimating & Bidding

Describe your estimating experience. What types of projects have you bid? What takeoff software have you used? Have you written scopes of work and leveled subcontractor quotes?



5. Actual Cost Reporting

Describe the most detailed cost report you have produced. What did it track? How frequently did you produce it? What software did you use?



6. CSI & NAICS Coding

Demonstrate your understanding of CSI coding — specifically where Division 9 falls and how you have applied it in practice. Describe your experience with NAICS coding in a construction context.



7. Architectural or Engineering Background

Do you have a degree or professional background in Architecture or Engineering? Describe it.



8. A Project You Are Proud Of

Describe one project you managed that you consider a genuine success — what was it, what were the challenges, and what was the outcome?



9. Availability & Rate

What hours are you available in U.S. Eastern Time? What is your target hourly rate?



10. The Filter

At the very top of your cover letter, write: "I build and I deliver."

Applications without this phrase will not be opened.



We do serious work. We want a serious Senior PM. If that is you — we look forward to hearing from you.

title: Claude AI Automation Engineer
description:
We are seeking a highly skilled Claude AI Automation Engineer who can design and deploy advanced AI automation systems using the Claude ecosystem, including Claude agents, Claude Co-Work, MCPs, Claude Skills, and custom API integrations.



This role is ideal for someone who deeply understands the Claude AI ecosystem and can leverage it for automation, programming, AI agents, and intelligent workflow orchestration to turn our company agentic.



Please apply here or
 



Role Overview:

The Claude AI Automation Engineer will architect and implement AI-driven automation systems using Claude AI and related technologies. This individual will help transform manual workflows into intelligent systems that can reason, execute tasks, and continuously improve operations.



You will work closely with leadership, engineering, and operations teams to build scalable AI infrastructure that supports automation across departments.



Key Responsibilities

• Design and implement AI-powered automation workflows using Claude AI

• Develop AI agents capable of reasoning, decision-making, and task execution

• Build automation systems that connect internal tools, APIs, and databases

• Implement Claude Co-Work environments to enhance team productivity

• Develop scripts and applications that integrate Claude with internal systems

• Build agentic workflows that can monitor, analyze, and execute operational tasks

• Optimize Claude prompts, reasoning chains, and memory structures

• Integrate Claude with automation platforms and orchestration tools

• Create internal AI tools that assist with documentation, analysis, and operations

• Identify opportunities across the company where AI can eliminate manual work

• Build scalable frameworks for AI-driven operations

• Maintain security and governance standards when deploying AI systems



Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Deep experience working with Claude AI and the Anthropic ecosystem

• Strong understanding of AI agent architectures and autonomous workflows

• Experience building AI automation pipelines



Proficiency in programming languages such as:

• Python

• JavaScript / Node.js

• Experience with API integrations and automation platforms

• Knowledge of prompt engineering, reasoning chains, and agent memory models

• Experience building or deploying multi-agent AI systems

• Strong problem-solving and system architecture skills



Preferred Experience:

• Experience with AI workflow automation tools

• Familiarity with n8n, Zapier, Make, or custom automation frameworks

• Experience building AI copilots or AI operational assistants



Knowledge of cloud environments such as:

• Microsoft Azure

• AWS

• Google Cloud

• Experience integrating AI into business operations and internal systems

title: Claude AI Integration Engineer
description:
We are seeking an experienced AI Integration Engineer to lead the implementation, integration, and operationalization of Claude (Anthropic) and ClawdBot across our business operations.



This role is not experimental. It is execution-driven.



You will design, deploy, and optimize enterprise-grade AI workflows that integrate Claude and ClawdBot into real business systems, including CRM, ticketing, cybersecurity operations, marketing automation, compliance documentation, reporting, and internal knowledge management.



The objective is measurable business impact, not surface-level automation.



Key Responsibilities:

• Architect and deploy Claude-based AI workflows across departments

• Integrate ClawdBot into internal systems and external client-facing workflows

• Build secure API integrations between Claude and our CRM, PSA, ERP, and BI systems

• Develop prompt engineering frameworks aligned with compliance and governance requirements

• Implement AI guardrails, logging, and role-based access controls

• Design automation workflows using tools such as n8n, Zapier, Power Automate, or custom scripts

• Create executive dashboards for AI utilization and ROI measurement

• Ensure all AI deployments align with cybersecurity and regulatory standards



Required Experience:

• Hands-on experience integrating Claude (Anthropic API) in production environments

• Experience deploying chatbot frameworks such as ClawdBot or similar LLM-based agents

• Strong API integration experience (REST, JSON, webhooks)

• Experience with workflow automation platforms

• Familiarity with prompt engineering methodologies

• Knowledge of AI governance, model limitations, and risk controls

• Experience working with enterprise data systems



Preferred Qualifications:

• Experience integrating AI into cybersecurity or compliance-driven environments

• Experience with Microsoft ecosystem tools (Power BI, Power Apps, Copilot)

• Knowledge of zero trust architecture principles

• Experience deploying AI in regulated industries (healthcare, finance, government)

• Python, Node.js, or similar scripting capability





Compensation:

• Competitive base salary

• Performance-based bonus tied to measurable AI impact

• Flexible engagement options (full-time, contract, hybrid)



Apply here and send resume to:

title: Sales Caller (Philippines – Remote) – Rockenue
description:
Great Base Salary + Uncapped Bonuses (Earn $600 per converted hotel lead)

About Rockenue



Rockenue is a hotel management company. We partner with hotels to take over and optimise their technology, marketing, sales, revenue management, and distribution. Our mission is to grow hotel revenue through better systems, stronger commercial strategy, and hands-on execution.

We help hotels increase occupancy, boost direct bookings, and reduce operational complexity.



Job Overview



We are seeking a confident Sales Caller from the Philippines to contact hotels in the UK and introduce them to Rockenue’s management services.

Your goal: identify interested hotels and deliver qualified leads to our team.



This is a fixed 1-month role, focused purely on generating leads.

Extension is only offered to high performers.



What You Will Do



Call UK hotels and introduce Rockenue’s full-service management offering



Deliver a clear 30-second pitch and speak to decision-makers



Qualify hotels that may benefit from our services



Pass qualified leads to our internal team



Keep clear records of calls and outcomes



Work during UK business hours



Qualifications



Excellent spoken English



Experience in sales, cold calling, or telemarketing



Hotel or hospitality industry experience is a strong advantage



Confident communicator able to manage objections



Reliable home office setup and stable internet



Target-driven, consistent, and proactive



Mandatory Video Submission: Please submit a short video answering:



Introduce yourself and briefly describe any relevant sales/calling experience



Give a 30-second mock pitch as if calling a UK hotel about Rockenue



Explain how you handle rejection or a hotel manager trying to end the call



Why you believe you’d be a strong fit for this role



Compensation



Attractive base salary for the 1-month fixed contract



Uncapped bonus: Earn USD $600 for every hotel lead that converts into a paying partner



Example: convert 10 hotels ? you earn $6,000 bonus



Contract extension possible for top performers

title: Spanish & English Speaking Receptionist
description:
About the role



We're looking for a sharp, personable Virtual Receptionist to serve as the face (and voice) of our team. You'll be the first point of contact for clients calling in, responsible for routing inquiries to the right department, owning our online access process, and keeping things running smoothly throughout the day.



This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, communicates with confidence, and takes genuine pride in creating a great client experience - even via video edits and admin tasks in the quieter 
nts.



What you'll do

-Answer inbound client calls - understand their needs and route them to the correct department efficiently and professionally

-Own the online access process end-to-end, guiding clients through it with patience and clarity (full training provided)

-Return voi
s and follow up with callers to ensure every client reaches the right team

-Assist with small administrative tasks — updates, data entry, light client support



What we're looking for

-Warm, professional phone presence and strong verbal communication skills

-Comfortable using cloud-based phone platforms (Aircall experience a plus, but not required)

-Highly organized — able to juggle calls, tasks, and follow-ups without dropping the ball

-Reliable internet connection and a quiet, professional home workspace

-Self-starter who can stay productive and focused during an independent work day and ASK for something if low call volume. You can always be learning to grow with the company.

-Prior experience in a receptionist, customer service, or administrative role preferred



*****13th month - pro-rated based on start date!



HOW TO APPLY: 

Priority - Gray, Low Priority = This must be chosen to have your application reviewed (we do use a filter to ONLY show people who submitted this)

Hours: 8 AM – 5 PM EST (Mon–Fri) = Answer phones as needed & send to the appropriate department

Light Admin Work - answering
s



What to expect in your first few weeks

*Onboarding and training on our online access process — no prior knowledge needed

*Shadowing calls to learn common client questions and routing paths

*Introduction to our admin tools, templates, and video editing workflow

*Support from our team as you get up to speed

title: Maintenance Coordinator for Property Management
description:
Altus Property Partners is a leading property management and real estate investment firm specializing in residential and multifamily properties. We are committed to maintaining high standards in property management by ensuring effective maintenance and prompt resolution of tenant, owners issues. We seek a dedicated Maintenance Coordinator to join our team and contribute to our mission of enhancing property value and tenant satisfaction. 



 Responsibilities: 



 Maintenance Coordination: 



- Oversee general maintenance and coordinate specialized vendor services as required. 

- Troubleshoot minor maintenance issues to determine if in-house staff or third-party vendors should be deployed. 

- Manage and schedule repairs, ensuring efficient resolution and high-quality service. 

- Utilize Property Meld to communicate directly with tenants and vendors, facilitating transparent and direct interaction. 



 Vendor and Project Management: 



- Source and evaluate vendors, obtaining estimates and adding new vendors to our systems. 

- Negotiate pricing where applicable, aiming to optimize costs while maintaining service quality. 

- Coordinate with in-house maintenance teams and external contractors to ensure timely completion of work. 



 Reporting and Performance Monitoring: 



- Monitor and report on key performance indicators such as speed of repair, tenant satisfaction ratings, response times, and cost management through Property Meld. 



 Tenant Relations: 



- Manage tenant interactions primarily through Property Meld to ensure all communications are logged and actionable. 

- Provide exceptional customer service, addressing tenant needs and ensuring satisfaction with maintenance efforts. 



 Emergency Management: 



- Handle maintenance emergencies during business hours, with management taking over after hours. 

- Develop protocols to ensure quick response and effective resolution of emergency maintenance issues. 

3

 Essential Qualifications: 



- Experience: Prior experience as a Maintenance Coordinator, preferably within a property management or real estate setting. Familiarity with general maintenance tasks and vendor management is essential. 

- Technical Skills: Proficiency in using property management software such as Buildium, Property Meld, and GoHighLevel CRM. 

- Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly convey repair needs and coordinate effectively between tenants, vendors, and management. 

- Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical abilities and problem-solving skills to handle maintenance issues efficiently and devise effective solutions. 

- Organizational Skills: Exceptional organizational and time-management skills, capable of managing multiple tasks simultaneously under tight deadlines. 



 Working Conditions: 



- This position is fully remote and requires the Maintenance Coordinator to be available during US Central Time hours. The role includes standard working hours with potential for occasional weekend and evening work to address urgent maintenance issues. 

  

 Compensation and Benefits: 



- Competitive salary based on experience 

- Opportunities for professional development and career growth 

- Supportive work environment focused on innovation and efficiency 



This is a full-time job.



US Central Standard Time Working Hours



Application Process: 



To get an interview, please send an
 to 
 (Exact
 subject: Altus Maintenance Coordinator) the following:

1. Summary of your work experience. Please highlight your executive/admin assistant experience.

2. PDF copy of your Resume/CV.

3. A screenshot of your internet speed.

4. A voice recording of a quick introduction of yourself.

title: Property Management Customer Service Representative
description:
Company: Altus Property Partners

Schedule: Full-time | Monday to Friday | 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM CST

Location: Remote (Philippines preferred)



About Us



Altus Property Partners is an investor-operated property management company that manages multifamily apartment buildings, single-family rental portfolios, and condominium associations. We combine best-in-class technology with a high-touch service model to deliver a superior experience for residents, owners, and board members alike.



We are now expanding our remote customer service team and are seeking a proactive, resourceful, and detail-oriented individual with a background in property management, real estate operations, or residential leasing.



About the Role



This role is critical to supporting our resident and client relationships. You’ll serve as a Customer Support Specialist who:



-Follows up with tenants, board members, and owners

-Gathers missing details and context through calls and
s

-Translates those conversations into actionable tickets and tasks for our internal teams

-Monitors and organizes incoming AI receptionist transcripts

-Assists with lease renewals, collections follow-ups, general support cases



This is not a passive call center position — we need someone who can think like an operator, ask the right questions, and move fast to get problems resolved or information routed accurately.



Key Responsibilities



-Handle incoming requests via AI-transcribed calls and
 channels (Buildium, GHL, etc.)

-Call residents and owners to clarify issues or requests

-Prioritize inquiries using the Eisenhower Matrix (Urgent vs. Important)

-Apply the 2-minute rule: complete immediately or create a documented task



Assist with follow-ups for:



-Maintenance tickets

-Lease renewals

-Resident complaints

-Owner communications

-Collections reminders



-Create task tickets and notes with accuracy and clarity

-Provide warm, articulate, and empathetic service to all callers

-Support coordination across departments (leasing, accounting, maintenance)

-Maintain professionalism while disqualifying vendor solicitations or spam calls



Must-Have Skills & Traits



Excellent spoken and written English (neutral or light accent preferred)

Strong emotional intelligence and ability to ask great questions

Can confidently speak with residents, owners, and contractors

Highly organized and action-oriented (finish or log every task)

Comfortable with CRMs, project management tools, and communication software, Property Management software



Willingness to learn our systems and SOPs quickly



Bonus: experience with Buildium, GoHighLevel, Microsoft Teams, or similar platforms



Ideal Background



2–4 years in property management support & customer service, or administrative

Familiarity with maintenance processes, lease renewals, or HOA operations

Past remote work experience with strong internet and equipment setup



Compensation and Benefits:



- Competitive salary based on experience

- Opportunities for professional development and career growth

- Supportive work environment focused on innovation and efficiency with other Philippine-based staff



This is a full-time job.



US Central Standard Time Working Hours

title: Call Center Agent
description:

We are looking for a dynamic Inside Sales Agent to join our digital marketing agency, which focuses on serving small businesses. As an extension of our clients, you'll be calling and texting prospects, booking appointments, and getting them excited about changing their lives. This is a remote position, requiring fluent, native English proficiency, and a clear accent. title: VSL Video Editor | 1000$, full time contract & long term
description:
Applications are only accepted via
. Replies to this job post will not be responded to.



About the role

We're not just looking for someone who can cut clips and add music. We're looking for a VSL editor who genuinely cares about the outcome — someone who watches a script and immediately starts thinking: how do I show this, not just tell it?

This is a long-term position on a structured, tight-knit team. If you're the kind of editor who gets excited by understanding a customer and figuring out what visuals will actually move them — read on.



What you'll be doing



Editing high-converting VSLs that pair strong scripts with the right visuals — not just stock footage, but intentional storytelling.

Spending real time understanding the target customer before you touch a timeline — their language, their fears, their desires.

Keeping viewers glued to the screen using pacing, pattern interrupts, text overlays, and dynamic cuts that serve the message.

Collaborating closely with our team — asking questions, sharing ideas, flagging things that could be stronger.

Continuously improving your workflow and staying up to date with what's actually working in direct-response video.





You'll thrive here if you



Don't just execute — you think. You push back when something could be better and bring ideas without being asked.

Understand that a great VSL is a sales tool first, and every editing decision should serve the conversion goal.

Are comfortable working within a structured environment with clear processes — no guesswork, no ambiguity.

Have a strong work ethic and take pride in the quality of what you ship, even on tight timelines.

Enjoy being part of a team, not just a freelancer working in a silo.

Are always looking to sharpen your craft — studying what converts, what keeps people watching, what doesn't.





What we offer



A structured team environment where you always know what's expected — no confusion, no dropped balls.

A stable, long-term working relationship with room to grow as we scale.

Honest feedback and real collaboration. We value your creative input.

A comfortable remote setup with clear communication and consistent workload.





How to apply

Send an
 to: 
 with the subject line "VSL Editor Application" and include:



2–3 VSL samples you're proud of (links preferred)

A short note (3–5 sentences) on how you approach a new VSL project before you edit a single frame

Your typical turnaround time and software of choice



We only accept applications submitted via
. Any replies left on job boards or social platforms will not be reviewed or responded to.

title: Experienced Medical Record Reviewer
description:
Prime Medical Evaluators is a leading medical-legal organization specializing in QME, IME, AME, and SIBTF evaluations. We support physicians by providing structured workflows, high-quality record organization, and defensible reporting standards.

We are seeking an experienced Medical Record Reviewer with a strong background in medical-legal or workers’ compensation cases to join our growing team.

This role is critical in ensuring physicians receive accurate, organized, and analysis-ready medical records to support high-quality evaluations.

Review, analyze, and organize complex medical records

Create chronological summaries and timelines of care

Identify key medical findings, diagnoses, and treatment patterns

Prepare record review summaries (RORs) for physicians

Flag missing records, inconsistencies, and critical issues

Work closely with physicians, case managers, and QA teams

Maintain strict HIPAA compliance and confidentiality



-Has experience in QME / AME / medical-legal records review

-Experience reviewing large volumes of records (hundreds to thousands pages)

-Strong attention to detail (correct dates, providers, facilities)

-Can work PST hours

-Can handle tight deadlines

-Can work independently with minimal supervision

-Understands confidentiality and HIPAA compliance

title: Gohighlevel Specialist
description:
Hey — I'm looking for an experienced GoHighLevel specialist for an ongoing project. This is for a course-based education brand in the equity/legal education space.



Here's what I need built:



CRM & PIPELINES

- Lead capture ? nurture ? enrollment pipeline

- Student lifecycle tracking (prospect ? enrolled ? alumni)

- Tag and segment contacts by course type, status, and engagement

- Private coaching pipeline to track students, documents, and deadlines



/SMS AUTOMATIONS

- Pre-enrollment nurture sequence 
 + SMS)

- Post-enrollment onboarding sequence

- Re-engagement campaigns for leads that go cold

- Webinar/event reminder sequences

- Abandoned cart / incomplete checkout follow-up



SALES FUNNELS & LANDING PAGES

- Main course enrollment funnel (opt-in ? VSL/webinar ? checkout)

- Lead magnet opt-in pages

- Thank you / confirmation pages

- Upsell/downsell pages for secondary offers



WEBSITE (future phase)

- Full website build inside GHL for the brand



Before we move forward, I need to know:



1. How many GHL builds have you completed? Can you share 2-3 examples?

2. Do you build funnels and automations from scratch or use templates?

3. Are you comfortable with Stripe/payment integration inside GHL?

4. What's your turnaround time for a full funnel + automation build?

5. Do you offer revisions and post-launch support?

6. What's your rate — per project, hourly, or monthly? 



This is a real business with active students and revenue. I'm not looking for a template drop. I need someone who understands conversion flow, writes clean automations, and can build this properly the first time.



If this is in your wheelhouse, send me your answers and examples and we'll go from there. Pay will be based on experience. Please also reference the number 44 in your response. 





Thanks.

title: Previous staff @ UberEats, Deliveroo, Glovo, etc.
description:
We are building a food delivery application for the Philippine market and are looking for experienced people who understand how the best platforms in the world are run. We need people who have lived it from the inside — not just used the apps, but worked within them. Your real-world experience will directly shape how we build our product.



Who we're looking for :



Previous or current employees of:

1. Uber Eats

2. Deliveroo

3. Glovo

4. Bolt Food

Or any other major food delivery service.



Roles we're especially interested in:

Customer support.





You'll advise our team on what makes these platforms tick behind the scenes — things like how customer complaints are resolved, how couriers and restaurants are onboarded, what processes actually work at scale, and where the biggest pain points tend to surface.



This is a consulting engagement, not a permanent role. You share your knowledge, we compensate you well, and you're done.
